postpn. (old H.) = se, q.v. The sixth letter of the Hindūstānī alphabet (the fourth of the Arabic) occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic. r: te, ta, Urdū, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, Hindī, t, abjad, ta-kār
def: s.m. The letter or sound t
more: is the third letter of the Arabic, and the fourth of the Persian alphabet. It is the 16th consonant in the Hindī alphabet, and the first letter of the तवर्ग or dental class. A termination for a verbal noun, as chalat movement, from chal go; banāvaṭ construction, from banā make. In the representation of Hindī words by the letters of these alphabets, it stands for the Nāgrī त. When used numerically, it denotes 400, according to the abjad, q. v . As an initial, medial and final, it is interchangable with d , as tāgā = dhāgā thread; toṅd = dhoṅd belly; tareṛ = daṛeṛ a fissure; kartā = kardā he does; H. bet = P. bed cane; palīt = P. palīd unclean; H. ant = Eng. end: with s , as te = se from; P. tez = sez sharp; thā = Panj. sā was; shahtūt = sasūt mulbery; H. mutte = musse with me; tetrā = tīsrā third; karat = Bhoj. karas doing; it = is this: with ch , as thopnā = chhopnā apply; tillā = chillā…

represents the Sanskrit or Hindi letter. ट. It does not occur in the Arabic or Persian alphabets; and its sound differs from that of تَ in being formed by pressing hard the tip of the tongue against the palate, instead of the roots of the teeth. It is the eleventh consonant of the Sanskrit and Hindi alphabet, and the first letter of the टवर्ग or cerebral class. It is convertible as an initial and medial with k , as ṭāëṅ ṭāëṅ = kāëṅ kāëṅ cawing; ṭhaḍā = khaḍā standing; anūṭhā = anokhā rare. As an initial with ch , as ṭīs = chīs pain; ṭītā = chīchā vulvæ; ṭoṅṭ = choṅch beak: with ḍ , as Tir. ṭabhkā = ḍabkā fresh water; tābar = ḍābar pond; E. ṭubakh = ḍubak guggle; ṭhīkrā = E. ḍhikṭā potsherd; ṭūṅḍī = E. ḍhoṅḍī navel: with t , q. v.: with d , as ṭhasak = dhasak a cough; with b , as ṭhainyā = bahinyā sister; ṭhanoī = bahnoī brother-in-law: with p , as ṭhaṭerā = phanṭerān the stalk of juār , …

in Arabic called tā, is the third letter of the Arabic and the fourth of the Persian alphabet. In the representation of Hindī words by the letters of these alphabets, it stands for the Nāgarī त or, with ه ( he ) subjoined, for थ . When used numerically, it denotes 400, according to the abjad, or appropriation of the letters of the Arabic alphabet to represent numbers. At the beginning of Arabic words, this letter is much used to form verbal nouns of various forms; as, تعريف from تالم ٫ عرف from تطاول ٫ الم from طول and, prefixed to a noun, it may be the تاٴالقسم denoting an oath; as, تالله By God. At the end of words in that language, moreover, it may be the تاٴتانيث sign of the feminine gender; as, ملکة A queen, from ملک A king: and, this termination may be adopted to restrict the noun to unity; as, تمرة A single date, from تمر: or, it may be used in the formation of infinitives or verb…
subs: vulg. ta-barg, s.m. The dental class of letters in the Nāgarī alphabet.

The thirteenth day after death when the last of the funeral rites is performed. Hin. tervīṅ sadī , n. f. The thirteenth century of the Mah. era; the kal jug or iron age.

more: adj. Crooked, bent, awry. ٿيڙها بيڙا ṭeṛhā beṛā, adj. Crooked. ٿيڙها کرنا ṭeṛhā karnā, To bend, to crook, distort.
subs: v.t. To misunderstand, misapprehend ( = ulṭā samajhnā)
(-ko), To give crooked answers, to give cross or rough answers, to be impertinent or rude (to)
v.t. To make crooked, to bend, crook, distort

wety: Inherited from Old Hindi टेढौ (ṭeḍhau), from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀢𑁆𑀭𑁂𑀟𑁆𑀟 (*treḍḍa).

def: adj. (dakh.) See ٿيڙها.

adj. 1. Crooked; bent; awry; askew; zig-zag. ' Haṅsuā re tūṅ ṭerh kāhe?' 'Ato apnā gauṅ se .' Rus. Prov. 'Why are you crooked, sickle, pray?' 'It suits my purpose every way.' 2. Forked; knock-kneed; bow-legged. 3. Hump-backed; hunch-backed. 4. Contrary; antagonistic; unfavourable; unpropitious. 1. Āj kal din ṭeṛhe haiṅ . There is a crook in his lot. Or, The times are unpropitious. 2. Amrit bikh kāraj karat, paṛī ṭeḍhā Bhagwān, Bikh amrit ho jāt hai, Īsh dayā parimānī . Dohā. Th' Almighty turned from you, to poison nectar turns; His lovet' wards you, to nectar poison quick returns. 5. Offended; displeased; angry. 1. Sir jhukāūṅ to aur ṭeṛhe ho . 2. Ṭeṛh jān sankhaë sab kāhū. Bakr chandramā girsaë na Rāhū . Ramayan. A crooked one to tackle, each one's heart misgave, As Rahu ventures not the horned moon to brave. 3. Kyā tumheṅ chiṛ mere salām se hai . Mīr. ṭeṛhā beṛā , adj. Altogether crooked; all manner of ways. ṭeṛhā-pan, ṭeṛh-panā , n. m. Crookedness, etc. ṭeṛhā samajhnā , v. n. To misapprehend ( ulṭā samajhnā ). ṭeṛhī sunānā , v. n. To give crooked or impudent answers. ṭeṛhī khīr , n. f. A dificult matter; a difficulty. Aisan ṭeṛh khīr ne khāib . Bhoj. None of this crooked khīr for me. ☞ A blind man once offered to treat his comrade, who had been blind from his birth, to a mess of milk and rice. His comrade, with the proverbial suspiciousness of the blind, inquired 'what is it like?' 'It is white,' said the other. 'And what is white like?' 'Like a crane.' 'What is a crane like?' 'Feel this,' said his would-be host, bending his forearm and hand from the wrist to imitate the crane's neck.' ṭeṛhā honā , v. n. 1. To be crooked. Mujh se ṭeṛhe ho-kar kyā loge? 3. To be displeased, angry. Abe! ṭeṛhā kyoṅ hotā hai? Slang.
s. f. Crookedness, obliquity.

def: adj. Sharp, keen, acute; penetrating, piercing (glance, &c.); hot, pungent, strong, acrid; caustic, corrosive; fiery, passionate, impetuous, violent; swift, fleet; quick, apt, intelligent, keen-witted; high-priced, dear
more: adj. 1. Sharp; pointed; acute; cutting. Phr.—Sharp as a needle, sharp as a razor. 2. Hot; warm; scorching. 3. Sharp; penetrating; piercing (eye). Baṛī tez nazar hai . 4. Pungent; strong; peppery; hot. Tataiyā mirch baṛī tez hotī hai . 5. Caustic; corrosive. 6. Hot; hot-headed; passionate; fiery. Inkā mizāj baṛā tez hai . 7. Swift; quick; fleet; impetuous. Ghoṛā kyā tez jātā hai! 8. Violent; great; excessive. Dāl meṅ namak tez hai . 9. Dear; high-priced ( maṅhgā ). 10. Clever; apt; quick; keen-witted. Yeh laṛkā sab meṅ tez rahā .

adj. Sharp, keen, pungent, acute, hot, fiery, caustick, acrid, strong, impetuous, violent, swift. تيزاب tez-āb, s. m. Nitrous acid, aqua fortis. تيزپات tez-pāt, s. m. Quickness of apprehension, intelligence, acumen, acuteness, aptness. تيزقدم يا گام tez-ḳadam or -gām, adj. Swift, active, quick, fast-going. تيزگامي tez-gām-ī, s. f. def: n. m. ( Chem .) An acid; aqua fortis tez honā , v. n. 1. To be sharpened. 2. To be thrown into a passion. Miyāṅ tez kyoṅ hote ho? lohā yā chhurī tez honā , v. n. 1. To be whetted or sharpened (iron or a knife). 2. To slay; to act the tyrant; to oppress. Sach hai, chhurī garīb pe hotī hai sab kī tez, Chheṛā sabā ne zulf ko, mujh par itāb hai . Mirzā. How sharp in truth the edge of swords to slay the poor are meant, The wanton breeze hath tossed your hair, on me your anger's spent.

def: n. m. S. स्थग a rogue. 1. An impostor; a cheat; rogue. 1. Ṭhag na dekhe, dekhe kalvār . Prov. If you have n't seen a ṭhag you have seen a publican. 2. Jis ne na dekhā ho bāgh, woh dekhe bilāī, Jis ne na dekhā ho ṭhag, woh dekhe qasāī . Prov. Who ne'er a lion saw a Tom cat sure hath seen, Than roadside strangler is a butcher worse, I ween! 3. Nā pīavā morā ṭhag, baṭ-parvā, na piavā morā chor, Nīndvā ke māt gaile saṛakyā pe soë, bābū Darogā jī! Mag. Wom. song. Jhūmar . To wait; linger; tarry; stop for; stay in expectation. Ṭhair-kar ānā . 5. To halt ( paṛnā , 2.); to put up ( ṭiknā 1.). Rūsal bahuṛyā, udgāral āg, Donoṅ ṭhaireṅ baṛe haiṅ bhāg . Prov. A wife offended, and a blazing fire, Good luck if out they do not go entire! 6. To last ( ṭiknā , 3). Aṅgrezī kapṛā nahīṅ ṭhairtā . 7. To be settled, fixed, agreed upon, struck (a bargain). 1. Pahle mazjūrī ṭhair jāë . 2. Lagāyā dāṅt jo lab pe, to haṅs ke farmāyā, Hamāre bose na ṭhāire, 'koī gizā ṭhairī? ' Zafar. With all a lover's passion when her lip he bit, She smiled and said, 'are kisses then a thing to eat?' 3. Luṭī daulat kī daulat, chūtyā ke chūtyā ṭhaire . Prov. His fortune spent, a fool transparent! 8. To be fixed; determined; resolved. 9. To bear up; endure; hold on. nutfa ṭhairnā , v. n. To be impregned (the semen); to conceive.

To strike a plate on the birth of a son, or a frying pan on the birth of a daughter, signifying that the first is born to eat and the last to cook.
s. f. 1. ( स्थाली ) A salver, a platter, a flat dish. 2. ( स्थल ) A mound about the root of a tree.
s. f. 1. A small flat metal or earthen dish. 2. (dakh.) Clapping or clap of the hands: see تالي tālī, sign. 2.

def: s.f. = thālī, q.v. s.f. A small flat plate or dish of metal or earth; plate, dish, platter; a salver; a stand (for a candlestick, or a ḥuqqa, &c.); (fig.) a present of sweetmeats, fruits, &c. (made by relations or friends on festive occasions)
subs: v.n. To strike or play on a thālī or plate.

def: adj. Loose, flabby, flaccid, soft, squashy; hanging loosely, baggy; shaky, waving, undulating, fluctuating (as a jelly, &c.); — s.f. Shaking loosely, being flabby, flabbiness, &c.

def: s.m. Support, prop, pillar, column, post; — thamb, thambh, the stem of a tree (esp. of a plantain); a pillar-shaped sweetmeat (offered at shrines chiefly by women); obstruction, obstacle, check, curb, anything that restrains.
